papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models Revisited
papers.abstract: We revisit scaling laws for large language models and find that compute-optimal training deviates from Chinchilla predictions at scale.

papers.title: Mixture of Experts Meets Instruction Tuning
papers.abstract: We demonstrate that Mixture of Experts architectures benefit disproportionately from instruction tuning, achieving 2x efficiency gains.

papers.title: Efficient Fine-Tuning with LoRA: A Comprehensive Study
papers.abstract: We provide a comprehensive evaluation of LoRA and its variants across 12 NLP benchmarks, finding that rank 16 is sufficient for most tasks.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.abstract: We extend Constitutional AI with AI-generated feedback for harmlessness, reducing reliance on human red-teaming by 60%.

papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization Without Reference Model
papers.abstract: We show DPO can be applied without a frozen reference model, simplifying the alignment pipeline while maintaining performance.

properties.evidence_quote: MoE architectures achieve 2x compute efficiency compared to dense models at the same parameter count.
Constitutional AI with AI feedback reduces human annotation by 60% while matching DPO alignment quality.

properties.assumption: MoE requires large batch sizes for efficient training
properties.evidence_quote: LoRA fine-tuning of MoE models works effectively with batch sizes as small as 4.
We confirm MoE instruction tuning gains reported in prior work across our evaluation suite.
DPO achieves competitive alignment without the compute budgets predicted by standard scaling laws.

gaps.description: Scaling law predictions for compute-optimal training are contradicted by DPO alignment results, but no study has reconciled these findings in a unified framework.
The intersection of MoE architectures and alignment techniques (DPO, Constitutional AI) remains under-explored. No paper directly compares these approaches on the same model family.
